sections.heading: Overview
sections.content: Primary bone tumors and selected metastatic tumors should be evaluated and treated by a multidisciplinary team (MDT) with expertise in the management of these tumors [TEAM-1]. The treatment philosophy emphasizes a multimodal approach integrating surgery, radiation therapy (RT), and systemic therapy as appropriate for the specific tumor type, grade, and stage. All recommendations are category 2A unless otherwise indicated.

sections.heading: MDT Requirements
sections.content: The core MDT should include: orthopedic oncologist, bone pathologist, medical/pediatric oncologist, radiation oncologist, and musculoskeletal radiologist [TEAM-1]. Specialists critical in certain cases include: thoracic surgeon, plastic surgeon, interventional radiologist, physiatrist, vascular/general surgeon, neurosurgeon/orthopedic spine surgeon, and palliative care physician. Additional surgical subspecialties may be included as clinically indicated.

sections.heading: Performance Status Guide
sections.content: Performance status (e.g., ECOG, Karnofsky) is a key factor in determining tolerance to aggressive multimodal therapy (e.g., neoadjuvant chemotherapy for osteosarcoma, Ewing sarcoma). For example, MAP chemotherapy is preferred for osteosarcoma patients <40 years with excellent performance status [BONE-B 4 of 7]. Palliative care and best supportive care are appropriate for patients with poor performance status.

nodes.label: Assess disease extent, tumor type, grade, and performance status

edges.label: Patients with localized bone sarcomas (e.g., osteosarcoma, Ewing sarcoma, localized chondrosarcoma, chordoma) where the goal is complete eradication of disease.
Patients with metastatic or unresectable disease where the goal is to extend survival, control symptoms, and improve quality of life.
Patients with high-grade localized disease (osteosarcoma, Ewing sarcoma) or resectable disease where pre- or post-operative systemic therapy is indicated.

cards.title: Curative Intent
cards.detail: Multimodal therapy is standard. For high-grade sarcomas like osteosarcoma and Ewing sarcoma, this involves neoadjuvant chemotherapy (where applicable), definitive local control (surgery and/or RT), and adjuvant chemotherapy. For chondrosarcoma and chordoma, surgery is the primary curative modality, with RT used as an adjunct or for unresectable disease.

cards.title: Palliative Intent
cards.detail: Treatment may include systemic therapy, palliative RT (including stereotactic techniques), and/or cytoreductive surgery/ablation for symptomatic sites. Enrollment in a clinical trial is strongly encouraged.

cards.title: Adjuvant/Neoadjuvant Intent
cards.detail: Neoadjuvant chemotherapy is preferred for high-grade osteosarcoma (category 1) and is standard for Ewing sarcoma. For Ewing sarcoma, at least 9 weeks of multiagent chemotherapy is recommended prior to local therapy (category 1). Adjuvant chemotherapy following local control is also standard for these tumors.
